creator: pluknet@gmail.com
text:
This is somewhat connected to kern/143521 (? Not sure), kernels are from one /usr/obj, though it's a different box with different setup.

I've got NMI on an almost idle system - FreeBSD 7.2-R amd64, in irq257: bce1.

db> bt 38       
Tracing pid 38 tid 100037 td 0xffffff0001add6e0
cpustop_handler() at cpustop_handler+0x40
ipi_nmi_handler() at ipi_nmi_handler+0x30
trap() at trap+0x31c
nmi_calltrap() at nmi_calltrap+0x8
--- trap 0x13, rip = 0xffffffff80513df4, rsp = 0xfffffffe80038ff0, rbp = 0xfffffffe80141a40 ---
_thread_lock_flags() at _thread_lock_flags+0x64
hardclock_cpu() at hardclock_cpu+0x7d
lapic_handle_timer() at lapic_handle_timer+0x132
Xtimerint() at Xtimerint+0x67
--- interrupt, rip = 0xffffffff80513e03, rsp = 0xfffffffe80141b80, rbp = 0xfffffffe80141bc0 ---
_thread_lock_flags() at _thread_lock_flags+0x73
ithread_loop() at ithread_loop+0x255
fork_exit() at fork_exit+0x11f
fork_trampoline() at fork_trampoline+0xe
--- trap 0, rip = 0, rsp = 0xfffffffe80141d30, rbp = 0 ---

(Again, the end part of backtrace is same as in kern/143521).

db> bt
Tracing pid 22 tid 100013 td 0xffffff0001970a50

fork_exit() at fork_exit+0x11f
fork_trampoline() at fork_trampoline+0xe
--- trap 0, rip = 0, rsp = 0xfffffffe800bad30, rbp = 0 ---

alltrace below.. kernel is in sorta locking SNAFU state.
